You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 48ffc6e2).

## Issue to Resolve

property-naming flags extension properties
The following code gets flagged with 0.49.1:

```kotlin
val Context.adsDelegateFactory @SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get() =
  applicationContext.getSystemService(SYSTEM_SERVICE_AD_DELEGATE_FACTORY) as AdsDelegateFactory
```

> Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed (standard:property-naming)

I would not expect this, if you're adding a property via an extension and leveraging `get()` since the value can indeed change inside the implementation.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

So when I remove `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")` it does not get flagged as expected. So there's something weird going on.
Indeed, the annotation causes the check to fail. Same happens with:
```
val Context.adsDelegateFactory private get() =
    applicationContext.getSystemService(SYSTEM_SERVICE_AD_DELEGATE_FACTORY) as AdsDelegateFactory
```
Problem will either be resolved in `0.49.2` only when more pressing bugs need to be fixed. Otherwise it will be fixed in the next normal release.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 26‚Üípublic class PropertyNamingRule :
 27‚Üí    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 28‚Üí    Rule.Experimental {
 29‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
 31‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33‚Üí    ) {
 34‚Üí        node
 35‚Üí            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 36‚Üí            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 37‚Üí    }
 38‚Üí
 39‚Üí    private fun visitProperty(
 40‚Üí        property: ASTNode,
 41‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 42‚Üí    ) {
 43‚Üí        property
 44‚Üí            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 45‚Üí            ?.let { identifier ->
 46‚Üí                when {
 47‚Üí                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 48‚Üí                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 49‚Üí                    }
 50‚Üí                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 51‚Üí                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 52‚Üí                    }
 53‚Üí                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 54‚Üí                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 55‚Üí                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 56‚Üí                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57‚Üí                    }
 58‚Üí                    else -> {
 59‚Üí                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 60‚Üí                    }
 61‚Üí                }
 62‚Üí            }
 63‚Üí    }

 74‚Üí            }
 75‚Üí    }

31‚Üípublic class SpacingAroundKeywordRule : StandardRule("keyword-spacing") {
32‚Üí    private val noLFBeforeSet = create(ELSE_KEYWORD, CATCH_KEYWORD, FINALLY_KEYWORD)
33‚Üí    private val tokenSet =
34‚Üí        create(
35‚Üí            FOR_KEYWORD, IF_KEYWORD, ELSE_KEYWORD, WHILE_KEYWORD, DO_KEYWORD,
36‚Üí            TRY_KEYWORD, CATCH_KEYWORD, FINALLY_KEYWORD, WHEN_KEYWORD,
37‚Üí        )
38‚Üí
39‚Üí    private val keywordsWithoutSpaces = create(GET_KEYWORD, SET_KEYWORD)
40‚Üí
41‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
42‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
43‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
44‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
45‚Üí    ) {
46‚Üí        if (node is LeafPsiElement) {
47‚Üí            if (tokenSet.contains(node.elementType) && node.parent !is KDocName && node.nextLeaf() !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
48‚Üí                emit(node.startOffset + node.text.length, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
49‚Üí                if (autoCorrect) {
50‚Üí                    (node as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
51‚Üí                }
52‚Üí            } else if (keywordsWithoutSpaces.contains(node.elementType) && node.nextLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
53‚Üí                val parent = node.parent
54‚Üí                val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
55‚Üí                if (parent is KtPropertyAccessor && parent.hasBody() && nextLeaf != null) {
56‚Üí                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
57‚Üí                    if (autoCorrect) {
58‚Üí                        nextLeaf.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
59‚Üí                    }
60‚Üí                }
61‚Üí            }
62‚Üí            if (noLFBeforeSet.contains(node.elementType)) {
63‚Üí                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
64‚Üí                val isElseKeyword = node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD
65‚Üí                if (
66‚Üí                    prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
67‚Üí                    prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
68‚Üí                    (!isElseKeyword || node.parent !is KtWhenEntry)
69‚Üí                ) {
70‚Üí                    val rBrace = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
71‚Üí                    val parentOfRBrace = rBrace?.treeParent
72‚Üí                    if (
73‚Üí                        parentOfRBrace is KtBlockExpression &&
74‚Üí                        (!isElseKeyword || parentOfRBrace.treeParent?.treeParent == node.treeParent)
75‚Üí                    ) {
76‚Üí                        emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
77‚Üí                        if (autoCorrect) {
78‚Üí                            (prevLeaf as LeafElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
79‚Üí                        }
80‚Üí                    }
81‚Üí                }
82‚Üí            }
83‚Üí        }
84‚Üí    }
85‚Üí}
86‚Üí

content.text:
The `PropertyNamingRule` in `ktlint` incorrectly flagged properties as needing screaming snake case even when they had a custom getter, if that getter had annotations or modifiers. This was because the rule's `hasCustomGetter()` check was too rigid, only looking at the first child of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node and expecting it to be the "get" keyword. 

If a getter has an annotation (e.g., `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")`) or a modifier (e.g., `private`), the first child of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node is a `MODIFIER_LIST`, not the `GET_KEYWORD`, causing the check to fail.

I have updated `hasCustomGetter()` to correctly identify a custom getter by searching for the `GET_KEYWORD` among all children of any `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` nodes associated with the property.

### Changes:
- Modified `PropertyNamingRule.kt`:
    - Added `GET_KEYWORD` to imports.
    - Updated `hasCustomGetter()` to iterate through `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` nodes and check for the presence of `GET_KEYWORD` within them, regardless of annotations or modifiers.

### Verification:
- Created a new test file `PropertyNamingRuleReproTest.kt` with test cases covering:
    - Extension property with an annotated custom getter.
    - Extension property with a private custom getter.
    - Top-level property with an annotated custom getter.
- Verified that all new tests pass.
- Verified that existing `PropertyNamingRuleTest` tests still pass.

```kotlin
// ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt

    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
        children()
            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR }
            .any { it.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null }
```

This fix ensures that properties with custom getters are correctly identified as such, even when they have annotations or modifiers on the getter, thus exempting them from the screaming snake case naming requirement when they are top-level or object properties.

 26‚Üípublic class PropertyNamingRule :
 27‚Üí    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 28‚Üí    Rule.Experimental {
 29‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
 31‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33‚Üí    ) {
 34‚Üí        node
 35‚Üí            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 36‚Üí            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 37‚Üí    }
 38‚Üí
 39‚Üí    private fun visitProperty(
 40‚Üí        property: ASTNode,
 41‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 42‚Üí    ) {
 43‚Üí        property
 44‚Üí            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 45‚Üí            ?.let { identifier ->
 46‚Üí                when {
 47‚Üí                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 48‚Üí                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 49‚Üí                    }
 50‚Üí                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 51‚Üí                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 52‚Üí                    }
 53‚Üí                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 54‚Üí                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 55‚Üí                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 56‚Üí                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57‚Üí                    }
 58‚Üí                    else -> {
 59‚Üí                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 60‚Üí                    }
 61‚Üí                }
 62‚Üí            }
 63‚Üí    }
 64‚Üí
 65‚Üí    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 66‚Üí        identifier: ASTNode,
 67‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68‚Üí    ) {
 69‚Üí        identifier
 70‚Üí            .text
 71‚Üí            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 72‚Üí            ?.let {
 73‚Üí                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 74‚Üí            }
 75‚Üí    }
 76‚Üí
 77‚Üí    private fun visitConstProperty(
 78‚Üí        identifier: ASTNode,
 79‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80‚Üí    ) {
 81‚Üí        identifier
 82‚Üí            .text
 83‚Üí            .takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 84‚Üí            ?.let {
 85‚Üí                emit(
 86‚Üí                    identifier.startOffset,
 87‚Üí                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 88‚Üí                    false,
 89‚Üí                )
 90‚Üí            }
 91‚Üí    }
 92‚Üí
 93‚Üí    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
 94‚Üí        identifier: ASTNode,
 95‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96‚Üí    ) {
 97‚Üí        identifier
 98‚Üí            .text
 99‚Üí            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
100‚Üí            ?.let {
101‚Üí                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
102‚Üí            }
103‚Üí    }
104‚Üí
105‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
106‚Üí        findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
107‚Üí            ?.firstChildNode
108‚Üí            ?.text == "get"
109‚Üí
110‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
111‚Üí
112‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
113‚Üí        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
114‚Üí            ?.children()
115‚Üí            .orEmpty()
116‚Üí            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
117‚Üí
118‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
121‚Üí
122‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
123‚Üí        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
124‚Üí            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
125‚Üí            containsValKeyword() &&
126‚Üí            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
127‚Üí
128‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =

134‚Üí            }

136‚Üí
137‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =

140‚Üí            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
141‚Üí            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
142‚Üí            .any { it.text == identifier }
143‚Üí

148‚Üí    }

31‚Üípublic class SpacingAroundKeywordRule : StandardRule("keyword-spacing") {
32‚Üí    private val noLFBeforeSet = create(ELSE_KEYWORD, CATCH_KEYWORD, FINALLY_KEYWORD)
33‚Üí    private val tokenSet =
34‚Üí        create(
35‚Üí            FOR_KEYWORD, IF_KEYWORD, ELSE_KEYWORD, WHILE_KEYWORD, DO_KEYWORD,
36‚Üí            TRY_KEYWORD, CATCH_KEYWORD, FINALLY_KEYWORD, WHEN_KEYWORD,
37‚Üí        )
38‚Üí
39‚Üí    private val keywordsWithoutSpaces = create(GET_KEYWORD, SET_KEYWORD)
40‚Üí
41‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
42‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
43‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
44‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
45‚Üí    ) {
46‚Üí        if (node is LeafPsiElement) {
47‚Üí            if (tokenSet.contains(node.elementType) && node.parent !is KDocName && node.nextLeaf() !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
48‚Üí                emit(node.startOffset + node.text.length, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
49‚Üí                if (autoCorrect) {
50‚Üí                    (node as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
51‚Üí                }
52‚Üí            } else if (keywordsWithoutSpaces.contains(node.elementType) && node.nextLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
53‚Üí                val parent = node.parent
54‚Üí                val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
55‚Üí                if (parent is KtPropertyAccessor && parent.hasBody() && nextLeaf != null) {
56‚Üí                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
57‚Üí                    if (autoCorrect) {
58‚Üí                        nextLeaf.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
59‚Üí                    }
60‚Üí                }
61‚Üí            }
62‚Üí            if (noLFBeforeSet.contains(node.elementType)) {
63‚Üí                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
64‚Üí                val isElseKeyword = node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD
65‚Üí                if (
66‚Üí                    prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
67‚Üí                    prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
68‚Üí                    (!isElseKeyword || node.parent !is KtWhenEntry)
69‚Üí                ) {
70‚Üí                    val rBrace = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
71‚Üí                    val parentOfRBrace = rBrace?.treeParent
72‚Üí                    if (
73‚Üí                        parentOfRBrace is KtBlockExpression &&
74‚Üí                        (!isElseKeyword || parentOfRBrace.treeParent?.treeParent == node.treeParent)
75‚Üí                    ) {
76‚Üí                        emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
77‚Üí                        if (autoCorrect) {
78‚Üí                            (prevLeaf as LeafElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
79‚Üí                        }
80‚Üí                    }
81‚Üí                }
82‚Üí            }
83‚Üí        }
84‚Üí    }
85‚Üí}
